query: IAEA Zaporizhzhia nuclear power plant off-site power loss 2026 cumulative count latest update
answer:
Excellent — now I have comprehensive, well-sourced data. Let me compile the final research report.

---

## IAEA Zaporizhzhia Nuclear Power Plant (ZNPP) Off-Site Power Loss: Cumulative Count & Latest Updates

### Key Finding: Cumulative Count

The **IAEA confirmed 11 complete losses of all off-site power** through **December 6, 2025** (IAEA Update 332), and at least **3 additional complete losses** occurred between late March and mid-May 2026 (IAEA Update 350), bringing the estimated cumulative total to approximately **14 complete losses of all off-site power** as of mid-May 2026.

---

### Confirmed IAEA Milestones

- **IAEA DG Grossi, UNSC briefing, 30 May 2023**: Stated off-site power loss had forced the use of emergency diesel generators on **"seven occasions"** since the start of the conflict.
- **IAEA Update 332, 6 December 2025**: "The ZNPP suffered yet another loss of all off-site power — **its 11th during the conflict** — lasting for about half an hour."
- **IAEA Update 350, 14 May 2026** (most recent update found): Since the main 750 kV Dniprovska line was disconnected on **24 March 2026**, the plant has experienced **"three total losses of off-site power"** (when the backup 330 kV Ferosplavna-1 line was also disconnected). This implies a cumulative total of approximately **14** complete losses.

---

### Chronology of Key Events

| # | Date | Event | Source |
|---|---|---|---|
| 1–7 | Aug 2022 – May 2023 | Seven complete off-site power losses requiring diesel generators | IAEA DG briefing, UNSC, 30 May 2023 |
| 8–10 | June 2023 – Sept 2025 | Additional events (details sparse) | IAEA updates |
| — | ~Late Sept – Late Oct 2025 | **Month-long off-site power loss** — the longest single event during the conflict | IAEA Update 332; Straits Times, 28 Sept 2025 |
| 11 | 6 December 2025 | Complete loss, 30 minutes; all EDGs started; restoration via 330 kV line in 29 min | IAEA Update 332 |
| — | 28 December 2025 | IAEA-brokered ceasefire for power line repairs | Wikipedia (ZNPP crisis article) |
| — | 10 February 2026 | 330 kV Ferosplavna-1 line disconnected; plant on single power source | IAEA Update 340 |
| — | 24 March 2026 | Main 750 kV Dniprovska line disconnected (still not repaired as of 14 May) | IAEA Update 345 |
| 12–14 | Late March – May 2026 | **Three complete off-site power losses** since 24 March | IAEA Update 350 |
| — | 30 May 2026 | Drone attack on Reactor 6 building; no radiation increase | IAEA statement, 31 May 2026 |

---

### Current Status (as of IAEA Update 350, 14 May 2026)

- **All 6 reactor units** remain in cold shutdown (since September 2022).
- The main **750 kV Dniprovska power line** has been disconnected since **24 March 2026** — over 7 weeks as of the update — and has **not been repaired**.
- The plant is reliant on a **single 330 kV backup line** (Ferosplavna-1), reconnected 5 March 2026 under the fifth IAEA-brokered ceasefire.
- Original capacity: **four 750 kV + six 330 kV lines**; most are now destroyed or inoperable.
- The IAEA has brokered **five temporary ceasefires**, enabling **six separate repairs** to power lines.
- Over **160 UAVs** were recorded flying near Ukrainian nuclear sites in a single 24-hour period.
- The electrical grid is described as being in its **worst condition** since IAEA began monitoring substations in September 2024.

---

### Infrastructure Context

- **20 emergency diesel generators** on-site; activate automatically during power losses.
- **Kakhovka Reservoir** (primary cooling source) eliminated by dam destruction on 6 June 2023; cooling now relies on **11 groundwater wells** and sprinkler ponds.
- IAEA has maintained **continuous on-site presence** (ISAMZ mission) since September 2022 (~45 months).
- Seventh IAEA mission to electrical substations completed (covering 14 substations).

key_facts: IAEA Update 332 (6 December 2025) explicitly confirmed the 6 December 2025 power loss was the 11th complete loss of all off-site power during the conflict.

IAEA Update 350 (14 May 2026, the most recent update found) states that since the main 750 kV Dniprovska line was disconnected on 24 March 2026, the plant has experienced three additional total losses of off-site power, bringing the estimated cumulative total to approximately 14.
By 30 May 2023 (IAEA DG Grossi UNSC briefing), the cumulative count was 7 complete off-site power losses requiring emergency diesel generators.
In autumn 2025 (~late September to late October), ZNPP experienced a month-long loss of all off-site power — the longest single such event during the conflict (IAEA Update 332).
As of 14 May 2026, the main 750 kV Dniprovska power line has been disconnected since 24 March 2026 (over 7 weeks) and has not been repaired; the plant relies on a single 330 kV backup line.
The IAEA has brokered five temporary ceasefires enabling six separate power line repairs.
The electrical grid is described as being in its worst condition since IAEA began monitoring substations in September 2024 (IAEA Update 334).
All 6 reactor units remain in cold shutdown since September 2022.
Before the conflict, ZNPP had four 750 kV and six 330 kV power lines; most are now destroyed or inoperable.
On 30 May 2026, a drone attack struck the Reactor 6 building; IAEA inspectors confirmed drone remains and a damaged metal hatch but no radiation increase.
Over 160 UAVs were recorded flying near Ukrainian nuclear sites in a single 24-hour period (IAEA Update 350).
